text: Ever felt someone else’s pain? What if your brain is mirroring theirs? Meet mirror neurons, the brain cells that fire when you act or watch someone act. They’re key to understanding actions, but can they fully explain empathy? Not quite. Research shows while they help us connect, empathy involves more complex brain functions. So, mirror neurons are part of the puzzle, but the full picture of empathy remains a mystery. Want to dive deeper into the mind? Hit like and follow for more insights!
